data.name: ApiPageCanonicalFieldNameGuideTitle
data.value: Give field more describing name.

data.name: ApiPageCanonicalFieldNameGuideMessageFormat
data.value: Consider naming field with a more descriptive name: '{0}'.

data.name: ApiPageCanonicalFieldNameGuideDescription
data.value: It is common for API pages to give certain fields a more describing name.

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyTitle
data.value: Redundant ApplicationArea on page

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyMessageFormat
data.value: ApplicationArea matches the page-level ApplicationArea and can be removed

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyDescription
data.value: When a ApplicationArea is defined on a page, it automatically applies to all fields of that page. Setting the same ApplicationArea again on individual fields is redundant and adds unnecessary noise to the object definition.

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove redundant ApplicationArea

data.name: AppManifestRuntimeBehindTitle
data.value: App manifest runtime is behind target version

data.name: AppManifestRuntimeBehindMessageFormat
data.value: The specified runtime version in app.json is falling behind. The project targets {0} version {1} and the current runtime is {2}. Update the runtime to {3} for compatibility with the latest runtime features.

data.name: AppManifestRuntimeBehindDescription
data.value: Specifying a runtime version in app.json is optional. When a runtime is explicitly defined and falls behind the targeted application or platform, newer language features and runtime capabilities may be unavailable, leading to unnecessary limitations or inconsistencies.

data.name: BuiltInDateTimeMethodTitle
data.value: Use new Date/Time/DateTime methods for extracting parts

data.name: BuiltInDateTimeMethodMessageFormat
data.value: Use the new method {0}.{1} to extract specific parts of date/time values.

data.name: BuiltInDateTimeMethodDescription
data.value: Replace outdated functions for extracting specific parts of Date, Time, and DateTime types (such as day, month, hour, or second) with the new, modernized methods.

data.name: BuiltInDateTimeMethodCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Use modern Date/Time/DateTime method

data.name: CognitiveComplexityMetricTitle
data.value: Cognitive complexity metric

data.name: CognitiveComplexityMetricMessageFormat
data.value: Cognitive Complexity: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: CognitiveComplexityMetricDescription
data.value: Reports the Cognitive Complexity score of a procedure or trigger. This diagnostic is informational and is always reported, independent of any configured threshold.

data.name: CognitiveComplexityIncrementTitle
data.value: Cognitive Complexity increment

data.name: CognitiveComplexityIncrementMessageFormat
data.value: {0}: +{1} (1 increment + {2} nesting penalty)

data.name: CognitiveComplexityIncrementDescription
data.value: Reports an individual Cognitive Complexity increment at the exact source location where complexity increases. Each diagnostic explains how the increment is composed, including the base increment and any additional nesting penalty. This diagnostic is intended to make the complexity calculation transparent and explainable.

data.name: CognitiveComplexityThresholdExceededTitle
data.value: Cognitive Complexity exceeds threshold.

data.name: CognitiveComplexityThresholdExceededMessageFormat
data.value: Cognitive Complexity: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: CognitiveComplexityThresholdExceededDescription
data.value: Indicates that the Cognitive Complexity of a procedure or trigger meets or exceeds the configured threshold. High Cognitive Complexity signals reduced readability and maintainability. Thresholds are configurable and should be interpreted in the context of the application domain and expected problem complexity.

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricTitle
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity metric

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricMessageFormat
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity: {0} (threshold ≥ {1})

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricDescription
data.value: Reports the cyclomatic complexity of a method or trigger. Cyclomatic complexity represents the number of independent execution paths through the code and provides insight into control-flow complexity.

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededTitle
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity exceeds configured threshold

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededMessageFormat
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity: {0} (threshold ≥ {1})

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededDescription
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity measures the number of independent execution paths through a method or trigger. When this value meets or exceeds the configured threshold, the code is considered overly complex, which can reduce readability, increase maintenance cost, and raise the risk of defects.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyTitle
data.value: Redundant DataClassification on field

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyMessageFormat
data.value: DataClassification matches the table-level DataClassification and can be removed.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyDescription
data.value: When a DataClassification is defined on a table, it automatically applies to all fields of that table. Setting the same DataClassification again on individual fields is redundant and adds unnecessary noise to the object definition.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove redundant DataClassification

data.name: ErrorInvocationUsingTextConstantTitle
data.value: Use Error with ErrorInfo or Label instead of Text

data.name: ErrorInvocationUsingTextConstantMessageFormat
data.value: Error is called with a Text value. Use ErrorInfo or a Label variable to preserve error details in telemetry.

data.name: ErrorInvocationUsingTextConstantDescription
data.value: Calling the Error method with a Text literal, Text variable, or Text expression (such as StrSubstNo) prevents the error message from being included in Business Central telemetry.

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerTitle
data.value: Explicitly set the RunTrigger parameter on build-in methods

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerMessageFormat
data.value: The method call '{0}' does not explicitly specify the RunTrigger parameter. Set RunTrigger explicitly instead of the implicit default value of false.

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerDescription
data.value: This rule enforces explicitly specifying the RunTrigger parameter when calling methods such as Insert, Modify, ModifyAll, Delete, and DeleteAll. These methods allow omitting the RunTrigger parameter, which implicitly defaults to false.

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersTitle
data.value: Use identifier syntax in event subscriber arguments

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersMessageFormat
data.value: Event subscriber arguments should use identifier syntax instead of string literals.

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersDescription
data.value: Starting with Business Central 2023 wave 1 (BC22), event subscriber arguments support identifier syntax rather than string literals. Using identifiers enables full Visual Studio Code navigation features such as Go To Definition and Find All References, improving code readability, refactor safety, and maintainability.

data.name: InternalProcedureNotReferencedTitle
data.value: The internal method is declared but never used.

data.name: InternalProcedureNotReferencedMessageFormat
data.value: The {0} method {1} in {2} {3} (Access = {4}) is declared but never used.

data.name: InternalProcedureNotReferencedDescription
data.value: The internal method is declared but never used. Unused procedures increase maintenance cost and reduce code clarity. Remove the procedure if it is no longer needed.

data.name: InternalProcedureOnlyUsedInCurrentObjectTitle
data.value: The internal method is only used in the object in which it is declared.

data.name: InternalProcedureOnlyUsedInCurrentObjectMessageFormat
data.value: The {0} method {1} is only used in the object {2} {3} (Access = {4}) in which it is declared. Consider making the procedure local.

data.name: InternalProcedureOnlyUsedInCurrentObjectDescription
data.value: This internal procedure is only referenced within its declaring object. Using internal visibility is unnecessary in this case. Making the procedure local reduces the exposed surface area and improves intent clarity.

data.name: InterfaceObjectNameGuideTitle
data.value: Use the standard 'I' prefix for interface object names.

data.name: InterfaceObjectNameGuideMessageFormat
data.value: Interface '{0}' must start with a capital 'I' and must not contain a space directly after it.

data.name: InterfaceObjectNameGuideDescription
data.value: Interfaces must follow the widely adopted naming convention of starting with a capital 'I' without any intervening spaces. This aligns AL interface naming with established conventions in languages such as C#.

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricTitle
data.value: Maintainability index metric

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricMessageFormat
data.value: Maintainability index: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricDescription
data.value: Reports the maintainability index of a method or trigger. The maintainability index is a composite metric derived from cyclomatic complexity, Halstead volume, and code size, providing an overall indication of how easy the code is to understand, modify, and maintain.

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededTitle
data.value: Maintainability index below configured threshold

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededMessageFormat
data.value: Maintainability index: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededDescription
data.value: The maintainability index provides an overall indication of code maintainability based on complexity, size, and structural characteristics. When this value falls at or below the configured threshold, the code is considered difficult to maintain, increasing the likelihood of defects and long-term maintenance cost.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationTitle
data.value: Do not use Object IDs as object references

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationMessageFormat
data.value: Object ID '{0}' used as object reference. Use the object name '{1}' instead.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationFormatWithoutCodeFix
data.value: Object ID '{0}' used as object reference.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationDescription
data.value: Using Object IDs in variable or property declarations reduces readability and prevents safe renaming. Object IDs are numeric and provide no semantic meaning, making the code harder to understand and maintain. Declare variables and properties using the resolved object name rather than a numeric Object ID.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationActionTitle
data.value: ALCops: Replace id with name of object reference

data.name: OptionTypeShouldBeEnumTitle
data.value: Prefer Enum over Option type

data.name: OptionTypeShouldBeEnumMessageFormat
data.value: Prefer using an Enum instead of an Option type.

data.name: OptionTypeShouldBeEnumDescription
data.value: Enums are preferred over Option types because they provide stronger typing, better extensibility, and clearer intent. Using Enums reduces error-prone patterns commonly associated with Option fields and improves maintainability and versioning of AL code.

data.name: PageStyleStringLiteralTitle
data.value: Use the new PageStyle datatype instead string literals

data.name: PageStyleStringLiteralMessageFormat
data.value: Avoid using the string literal '{0}' for page styling. Use the PageStyle datatype instead (PageStyle::{1}).

data.name: PageStyleStringLiteralDescription
data.value: Adopting the use of the new PageStyle datatype allows to more easily get the supported pagestyles via IntelliSense and avoids incorrect behaviour when a typo is made in hardcoded strings or label variables.

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelTitle
data.value: Use ReadIsolation instead of LockTable

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelMessageFormat
data.value: Use ReadIsolation(IsolationLevel::UpdLock) instead of LockTable().

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelDescription
data.value: Use the ReadIsolation method to control isolation level on database transactions instead of the LockTable method.

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelCodeActionTitle
data.value: ALCops: Replace LockTable() with ReadIsolation

data.name: UseIsEmptyMethodInsteadOfCountTitle
data.value: Use Rec.IsEmpty() for checking record existence

data.name: UseIsEmptyMethodInsteadOfCountMessageFormat
data.value: Avoid using {0}.Count() for checking record existence. Use {0}.IsEmpty() instead for better performance.

data.name: UseIsEmptyMethodInsteadOfCountDescription
data.value: To check for the existence of records, use the more efficient Rec.IsEmpty() function instead of Rec.Count().

data.name: UseQueryOrFindWithNextInsteadOfCountTitle
data.value: Consider using a Query object or Rec.Find('-') with Rec.Next() for checking exactly one record

data.name: UseQueryOrFindWithNextInsteadOfCountMessageFormat
data.value: Consider using a Query object or {0}.Find('-') together with {0}.Next() instead of {0}.Count() for faster and more efficient record checks.

data.name: UseQueryOrFindWithNextInsteadOfCountDescription
data.value: Instead of relying on Rec.Count(), consider using a Query object or a combination of Rec.Find('-') and Rec.Next() for faster and more efficient record checks.

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextTitle
data.value: Use SecretText type to protect credentials and sensitive textual values from being revealed

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextMessageFormat
data.value: Sensitive textual values should be passed as SecretText to prevent them from being revealed.

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextDescription
data.value: Using SecretText prevents confidential information such as API keys, tokens, passwords, and similar secrets from being exposed through the AL debugger during regular or snapshot debugging.

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadTitle
data.value: Use partial records on read operation

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadMessageFormat
data.value: Use SetLoadFields before '{0}.{1}()' to improve performance by loading only the fields that are needed.

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadDescription
data.value: When reading records from the database using Get, Find, FindFirst, FindLast, or FindSet, use SetLoadFields to specify which fields to load. This avoids loading all normal fields (including those from table extensions), reducing SQL joins and data transfer. For performance reasons, it is not recommended to use partial records on a record that will do inserts, deletes, renames, field transfers, or copies to temporary records.

data.name: UsePartialRecordsOnReadCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Add SetLoadFields

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsRedundancyTitle
data.value: Redundant AllowInCustomizations on field

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsRedundancyMessageFormat
data.value: AllowInCustomizations matches the table-level AllowInCustomizations and can be removed.

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsRedundancyDescription
data.value: When AllowInCustomizations is defined on a table or tableextension, it automatically applies to all fields. Setting the same AllowInCustomizations again on individual fields is redundant and adds unnecessary noise to the object definition.

data.name: AllowInCustomizationsRedundancyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove redundant AllowInCustomizations

data.name: TranslatableTextShouldBeTranslatedTitle
data.value: Translatable text should be translated

data.name: TranslatableTextShouldBeTranslatedMessageFormat
data.value: Missing translation for '{0}' in language(s): {1}

data.name: TranslatableTextShouldBeTranslatedDescription
data.value: All translatable texts (captions, tooltips, labels, option captions) should have translations in the project's XLIFF files for every target language. Missing translations cause the application to display the source language text to users in other locales, degrading the user experience.
